Description: The Tempo Skeleton Dame reveals the heart of Swiss watchmaking, where refinement meets emotion. Its open-worked architecture showcases the automatic movement, a visible ballet of gears and springs. Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. pages.title: Worden Via Negativa
pages.content:
Product: Worden Via Negativa
Brand/Vendor: WORDEN
Type: Watches
Price: $900.00 CAD
URL: https://watchdna.com/products/007-via-negativa-7-25
Tags: Premium Brands
Description: Limited run of just 25 pieces — with several already sold. "Via Negativa" is a Latin term that translates to "the way of negation." In philosophy and theology, it refers to a method of approaching truth or understanding by defining what something is not rather than by affirming what it is. In design, via negativa is essentially the study of what not to do, and this methodology was used extensively in the development of Worden Watch. This approach can be applied to any creative process and will resonate with artists, free thinkers, and designers alike.

Description: Limited to 200 pieces annually: In 2019, MICROMILSPEC launched a test program by distributing 30 timepieces to military personnel within the Norwegian Armed Forces. The goal: to rigorously test the watches in the field and report back on performance and accuracy. These watches were purposefully designed and stripped of all decorations from the bezel, crown, and caseback. A deliberate choice allowing users to easily trace and document their watch’s lifelines. Description: A minimalistic multifunction with a sleek and modern feel, the BOSS Tyler reflects the focus and determination of every BOSS. Angled, brushed lugs add an architectural feel to the watch, which is fitted with a choice of mesh bracelet, link bracelet, or leather strap. Framed by a slim polished bezel, the super-matte dial is detailed with stepped-down day and date sub-eyes, while a range of color combinations ensure the versatility of this modern timepiece.

Description: This remarkable wristwatch is part of the Heritage Collection which celebrates Luminox’s 35 years of groundbreaking light technology. For maximum visibility, this timepiece features 16 LLT tubes and a white-textured dial fully coated with Super-LumiNova®, which gives it an aquatic glow when charged and activated in dark light conditions. In addition, the numbers on the bezel are painted with Super-LumiNova®, making this watch Luminox’s most luminous piece to date. Description: For the first time, Mido has created a skeleton Swiss watch especially dedicated to women. The new and audacious Rainflower Blossom features delicate lines, directly inspired by the lotus flower shape of Singapore’s contemporary ArtScience Museum. Resolutely feminine petals are interlaced along the edges. Adorned with an openwork flower, the captivating dial reveals at its centre the Caliber 80 – the latest-generation automatic movement, whose mainplate has been specially skeletonised and decorated. It invites the wearer to marvel at the passing of time with audacityand elegance.

Description: The Ocean Star 600 Chronometer is a superlative diving watch that embodies the wealth of MIDO technological expertise. The COSC-certified Caliber 80 Si combines an exceptional autonomy of up to 80 hours of power reserve with extraordinary accuracy and shock-resistance thanks to its silicon balance-spring. Equipped with a helium valve, this diving watch withstands a pressure of up to 60 bar (600 m/1,968 ft). The ceramic bezel ring features numerals with Super-LumiNova Grade X®, offering ultra-powerful and long-lasting luminescence to ensure perfect readability in the ocean’s depths.

Description: With a sixties-inspired diver's watch look and a stainless steel case measuring 36.5 mm in diameter, it adapts and adopts all the wrists. Ergonomic and sporty, the Ocean Star 36.5 combines the codes of diving watches with a refined aesthetic while defying time with its Swiss automatic movement that allows up to 80 hours of autonomy. A screw-down case back contributes to the piece's extraordinary water resistance, up to a pressure of 20 bar.

Born from MIDO's extraordinary heritage, the TV-shaped case returns to the centre stage for the Multifort TV Big Date, as intense as it is precise, thanks to its formidable mechanical movement. The modern and dynamic design of this Swiss watch is underlined by a striking horizontal brushed finish dial and, along wtih its distinctive function: the BIG DATE at 12 o'clock. Description: With its retro look and extreme reliability, the Multifort Powerwind pays a stylish tribute to Mido’s expertise. In 1954, the “Powerwind” movement was first produced, giving rise to a revolutionary model which bears its name. At the time, this automatic movement it's Midos's most precise and reliable, which represents a great evolution. It is with this same ambition that Mido continues to equip the Swiss watch Multifort Powerwind with a high technology that allows to offer timekeeping of unparalleled reliability and precision, as in the past.

pages.content:

Brand/Vendor: MIDO

Tags: 10 bar (100 m / 330 ft), 42, Automatic, Grey gradient, Men, Multifort, Power reserve up to 80 hours
Description: The Multifort M Chronometer with a movement tested and certified by the COSC (Contrôle Officiel Suisse des Chronomètres). This model is distinguished by its exceptional quality – well beyond the standards. Description: With the Commander Gradient, watchmaking mechanics become visible thanks to a transparent acrylic dial – a first in this iconic collection. As such, the components of the Caliber 80 can be admired on the dial side as well as on the rear, thanks to a transparent case back. Tags: 43.5, 60 bar (600 m / 1968 ft), Automatic, Black, Men, Ocean Star, Power reserve up to 80 hours
Description: The Ocean Star 600 Chronometer is a superlative diving watch that embodies the wealth of MIDO technological expertise. The COSC-certified Caliber 80 Si combines an exceptional autonomy of up to 80 hours of power reserve with extraordinary accuracy and shock-resistance thanks to its silicon balance-spring. Equipped with a helium valve, this diving watch withstands a pressure of up to 60 bar (600 m/1,968 ft). The ceramic bezel ring features numerals with Super-LumiNova Grade X®, offering ultra-powerful and long-lasting luminescence to ensure perfect readability in the ocean’s depths.

Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. Description: Original aesthetics for an offbeat look. The model is available here with wood and cowhide. It is the ash tree from the vicinity of Praz-sur-Arly that is highlighted for the dial. This tree has an exceptional quality of resistance that is used in the construction of alpine chalets, the very symbol of the mountain. It is cut, sawn and worked on site. These boards, when heated, take on this beautiful caramel-brown color. For a more classic style, a mahogany Ingrassata cowhide leather strap is in harmony.

Description: The Réti Mark II is a tribute to the iconic chess game between Richard Réti and Savielly Tartakower, played in 1910. Tags: Premium Brands
Description: The Flieger connects over 40 years of aviation heritage with the modern era. The Tactical Green and Mission Black Flieger models are limited to just 100 pieces each worldwide and finished with the ultimate state-of-the-art Swiss Diamond-Like-Carbon (DLC) Coating. With a 70-hour power reserve, the build in Werk 7 delivers unwavering reliability no matter the conditions. Description: The RESERVOIR 390 Fastback collection is inspired by the speedometer of the 1967 American "Pony Car". Description: RESERVOIR applies the measuring of sound to the measuring of time ! The two retrograde hands of the new RESERVOIR Sonomaster Chronograph faithfully reproduce the hands of power measurements or VU meters from analog stereo amplifiers. A tribute to the human quest for a 'pure' sound, a musical exploration full of audacity and intensity. This bi-retrograde (date and seconds at 120°) watch inaugurates the first chronograph model from RESERVOIR (central second, 30-minute counter at 12, hour counter at 6). The watch is powered by the new watchmaking caliber RSV-Bi120: a manufacture bi-retrograde ...

pages.content: Watch Brands Guide: Complete Collections & Reviews – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Brands DNA GROUPS COUNTRIES CONNECTED ALPHABETICAL BRANDMARK GROUP CASIO COMPUTER CO. LTD CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT FESTINA GROUP FOSSIL GROUP INC FRANCK MULLER GROUP ICE HOLDING GROUP INVICTA WATCH GROUP KDDL GROUP LVMH MONDAINE GROUP MOVADO GROUP PARTNERS GROUP PIERRE LANNIER GROUP POINT TEC RIBA WATCH GROUP ROLEX SA SEIKO GROUP CORPORATION SWATCH GROUP LTD TIMEX GROUP TIMEWAY GROUP FINE JEWELLERY INDEPENDENT WATCH COMPANIES MICROBRAND AUSTRALIA AUSTRIA BELGIUM CANADA CHINA DENMARK ENGLAND FRANCE GEORGIA GERMANY GREECE HONG KONG ITALY INDIA IRELAND JAPAN LITHUANIA NETHERLANDS NORWAY PUERTO RICO ROMANIA SINGAPORE SPAIN SWEDEN SWITZERLAND THAILAND TAIWAN UNITED KINGDOM UNITED STATES OF AMERICA TRADITIONAL BRANDS × Breitling Breitling is a Swiss luxury watch company that was founded in 1884 by Leon Breitling. The company’s headquarters are located in Grenchen, Switzerland. Breitling is known for producing high-quality, precision watches for pilots, divers, and other professionals. The company’s watches are known for their durability, accuracy, and distinctive design, and are highly sought after by collectors and enthusiasts. In addition to its headquarters in Switzerland, Breitling has boutiques and authorized dealers located in cities around the world. Read More × Girard Perregaux The Girard-Perregaux watch brand was established in 1791, making it one of the oldest luxury watch manufacturers in Switzerland. The brand is well-known for its sophisticated watches as well as the technological improvements that it has made during its long history of invention and excellence in the watchmaking industry. During the course of its history, the brand is responsible for a number of significant advancements in horology, including the invention of the wristwatch and numerous other significant timekeeping advances. The timepieces produced by Girard-Perregaux are renowned for their unmatched elegance, outstanding craftsmanship, and meticulous attention to detail. Collectors and watch aficionados all around the globe have an insatiable appetite for the brand’s collections, which include everything from traditional dress watches to timepieces designed for sports and diving. Read More × Hublot Hublot is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1980 by Carlo Crocco. The brand is known for its bold, contemporary designs, and its use of advanced materials, such as carbon fiber and ceramic. Hublot is particularly well-known for its “Big Bang” collection, which features watches with a distinctive, layered case design, as well as its “Classic Fusion” collection, which is inspired by traditional watchmaking styles. Hublot is also known for its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities, such as Ferrari, and Pelé, and for its commitment to traditional watchmaking techniques. Hublot watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their unique style, and craftsmanship. Read More × Ice Watch Ice-Watch is a bold and accessible watch brand that emerged in the entry-level fashion timepiece segment. Founded by Jean-Pierre Lutgen in late 2006 and distributed globally since 2007, it fills the niche for colourful, stylish watches that feel both youthful and universally appealing. Known for its vibrant blend of retro-modern design and versatility, Ice-Watch offers a wide variety of styles—from sporty and minimalist to glamorous, urban, and vintage—crafted to suit a diverse audience spanning children to seniors. Its unique selling points include strong visibility through celebrity endorsements (like David Guetta, Katy Perry, Eden Hazard), partnerships with brands such as BMW Motorsport and Pantone, and a fashion-forward yet affordable positioning Read More × Louis Vuitton Louis Vuitton is a French luxury maison specializing in leather goods, fashion, accessories, and high-end watchmaking. Renowned for its artisanal heritage and iconic retro-contemporary design (including the emblematic monogram and hardware inspired by its historic trunks), the brand blends timeless elegance with modern innovation. It appeals to a global clientele who value exclusivity, exceptional craftsmanship, and the ability to express personal style through luxury. In the watchmaking field, Louis Vuitton embodies a unique fusion of travel spirit, creativity, and horological savoir-faire. Since the launch of its emblematic Tambour collection in 2002—with its distinctive drum-shaped case—the brand has expanded into fine watchmaking, combining bold aesthetics with technical mastery. Highlights include Escale (with refined proportions, trunk-inspired details, and in-house movements), Spin Time (using rotating cubes instead of hands), Voyager, and Horizon (smartwatches tailored to modern travel). These timepieces target both luxury connoisseurs and contemporary travelers seeking exceptional design with functional innovation. Read More × Montblanc Montblanc is a luxury watch and writing instrument brand founded in Germany in 1906. Known for its high-quality craftsmanship, Montblanc watches feature elegant designs and precision engineering. The brand offers a wide range of watches for both men and women, including classic timepieces, sport watches, and limited edition collections. In addition to watches, Montblanc also produces writing instruments, leather goods, and jewelry. Read More × Omega Omega is a Swiss luxury watch brand that has been synonymous with quality, accuracy and innovation for over a century. Founded in 1848, Omega has since become one of the world’s most respected and celebrated watchmakers, thanks in part to its rich heritage of technical excellence and its commitment to pushing the boundaries of watchmaking. Known for its iconic designs and legendary timepieces such as the Speedmaster and the Seamaster, Omega has earned a reputation as a watchmaker that combines classical style with cutting-edge technology to create timeless and enduring timepieces. Read More × Seiko Seiko is a Japanese watch brand that was founded in 1881. It is one of the oldest and most respected watch manufacturers in the world. Seiko is renowned for its innovation and advanced technology in the watch industry, including its development of quartz watches and kinetic watches. The brand offers a wide range of watches, from sporty and casual to elegant and sophisticated, catering to every watch enthusiast’s needs and styles. Seiko’s commitment to high-quality craftsmanship, precision, and durability has made it a popular choice among watch enthusiasts worldwide. Read More × TAG Heuer TAG Heuer is a luxury watch brand based in Switzerland. The company was founded in 1860 by Edouard Heuer. The brand is known for its sporty, stylish watches, and its use of advanced technology, such as chronographs and quartz movements. TAG Heuer is particularly well-known for its Carrera and Monaco collections, which are inspired by the world of motor racing, and its Link collection, which is characterized by its iconic bracelet design. The brand is also known for its collaborations with other luxury brands and personalities, such as McLaren and Leonardo DiCaprio and for its commitment to precision and innovation. TAG Heuer watches are highly sought-after by collectors and enthusiasts for their sporty, stylish design and craftsmanship. Read More × Tissot Tissot stands as a titan in the realm of watchmaking, boasting a heritage that stretches back to 1853 when it was founded in Le Locle, Switzerland, by Charles-Félicien Tissot and his son, Charles-Émile Tissot. Renowned for its pioneering spirit and innovation, Tissot has been at the forefront of many breakthroughs in the watch industry, including the creation of the first mass-produced pocket watch and the first watch with two time zones in the late 19th and early 20th centuries. As part of the Swatch Group since 1983, Tissot has continued to expand its reputation for quality craftsmanship at accessible prices, embracing modern technologies and materials. Its landmark introductions include the T-Touch, the first tactile watch, underscoring Tissot's commitment to innovation. Today, Tissot's legacy is evident in its diverse range of timepieces, from elegant traditional watches to high-tech sports models, making it a brand celebrated worldwide for its precision, reliability, and distinction. Read More TECH BRANDS × Apple Apple Inc. is a premium consumer electronics and lifestyle technology company specializing in hardware, software and services. Though Apple Watch is featured here, the company is globally known for the iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple TV, and Apple Watch—a seamless ecosystem combining sleek minimalist design with advanced functionality. Its design philosophy pairs retro-modern fusion—clean, refined aesthetics with cutting-edge materials like recycled aluminum, titanium, and sapphire crystal—and a focus on privacy, sustainability and intuitive usability. Apple Watch products balance fashion and fitness, offering health insights, safety features like Crash Detection and sleep apnea notifications, and long battery life across different models. Read More × Garmin Garmin Ltd. is a pioneering technology company in GPS‑based consumer electronics, specializing in navigation and wearable devices. The brand is renowned for combining practical innovation with robust design, targeting outdoor enthusiasts, athletes, pilots, mariners, and everyday users who value accuracy and durability. Known for reliable GPS and sensor‑based products (like its iconic Forerunner running watches and Marine chartplotters), Garmin blends high‑precision engineering with user‑centered design. Its devices often feature rugged form factors, long battery life, and intuitive interfaces—all built for use in challenging environments. Garmin’s key product categories include fitness wearables, marine electronics, aviation instruments, outdoor handhelds, and automotive GPS units. Read More × Google Google is a premium consumer electronics brand specializing in smartwatches, particularly the Pixel Watch series. Known for seamlessly combining Google’s software intelligence with Fitbit’s health‑tracking expertise, the brand integrates circular, domed-glass designs with advanced sensors and AI‑powered functionality. Their design philosophy emphasizes elegant, minimalist aesthetics—the stainless-steel, water-resistant (5 ATM) Pixel Watch features a bold, domed display and a proprietary band‑attach mechanism inspired by camera lens mounts. This allows easy customization with a variety of woven, stretch, active, leather, and metal bands. Key products include the first‑generation Pixel Watch (launched October 2022), Pixel Watch 2 (October 2023), and Pixel Watch 3 (September 2024), each improving health‑tracking, display area, battery life, and Wear OS integration. The audience includes tech‑savvy Android users, fitness-conscious individuals, and existing Pixel ecosystem customers seeking stylish yet powerful smartwatches with deep Google services integration. Read More × Link2Care Link2Care is a forward-thinking health technology company specializing in smart wearable devices that integrate Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) with Western medical practices. Their flagship product, the Watch2Care, is a 4G-enabled hybrid smartwatch that acts as an independent hub, connecting various IoT devices to provide comprehensive health monitoring. This integration allows for the collection of both Western biometric data and TCM pulse information, offering a holistic view of the user's health in real-time. The accompanying Link2Care app processes this data to generate a daily health score, delivering personalized, actionable insights to users. Additionally, the platform connects users to the Link2Care Alliance, a curated marketplace of validated supplements, ensuring that users have access to effective wellness products. Endorsed by the Federation of Hong Kong Watch Trade and Industries Ltd., Link2Care aims to set new standards in health monitoring through interoperability and strategic partnerships. Read More × Porsche Porsche Design is a luxury minimalist lifestyle brand rooted in industrial design, founded in 1972 by Professor Ferdinand Alexander Porsche—the designer of the iconic Porsche 911. The brand started with the Chronograph 1, widely recognized as the first all-black wristwatch, and remains a cornerstone of its high‑precision timepiece collection. The brand’s philosophy emphasizes purist aesthetics and honest design, with the guiding principle: “If you analyze the function of an object, its form often becomes obvious.” Porsche Design products—ranging from watches, eyewear, and luggage to electronics and home accessories—combine sleek, retro‑modern forms with functional innovation and premium materials ℝ (e.g. titanium, stainless steel), catering to discerning consumers who value craftsmanship, restraint, and engineering excellence. Read More × Samsung Samsung is a global electronics leader specializing in wearable devices—particularly the Samsung Galaxy Watch series, sold in Canada through its official channel. The brand emphasizes a sleek fusion of modern technology and classic watch aesthetics, combining Samsung’s hallmark circular bezel designs with cutting‑edge smart features like AI‑powered wellness tracking and smart home integration. As a pioneer in health‑focused wearables, Samsung offers devices that blend performance with style. Its latest models—such as the Galaxy Watch8 Classic and Ultra—feature high‑performance 3 nm processors, rotating bezels, and seamless integration with Samsung Health and SmartThings for fitness, sleep coaching, and smart‑device control. Samsung targets tech‑savvy consumers who want the functionality of a smartwatch with the look and feel of a timeless timepiece. Read More × Withings Withings is a French innovative health-tech and connected consumer electronics brand specializing in smart devices that help users monitor, track, and improve their overall health and wellbeing. Known for pioneering early connected health products — such as the first Wi-Fi scale on the market — the brand combines sleek hardware design with intuitive software and data insights, enabling individuals to track vital metrics like weight, activity, sleep, heart rate, blood pressure, and more in a unified experience. Withings’ design philosophy centers on creating technology that feels beautifully simple, useful, and personal — from analog-style hybrid smartwatches that resemble traditional timepieces to advanced biometric scales and FDA-cleared medical-grade monitors. Its ecosystem is tied together by the Health Mate app, which aggregates data from Withings devices to offer trends, coaching, and insights that help users make informed health decisions. The brand’s key products include smart scales (such as Body+ and Body Scan), hybrid smartwatches (like ScanWatch and Move ECG), blood pressure monitors, thermometers, sleep tracking systems, and companion software. Withings targets health-conscious consumers, tech-savvy individuals, and people interested in preventive and holistic health monitoring, offering devices that blend everyday wearability with meaningful, clinically relevant measurements. Its unique selling points include award-winning design, market-first innovations, and integration across platforms for a cohesive health experience. pages.content: About WatchDNA | Educate, Entertain & Connect Watch Enthusiasts Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Your browser does not support the video tag. We aim to build a vibrant community where people can discover new brands and meet jewellers and aficionados passionate about timepieces and their stories. Here at WatchDNA, we recognize how essential it is to have all the knowledge required before purchasing. While our goal is to promote the item sold by our jewellery and brand partners, we strongly encourage everyone to learn as much as possible about each brand. Our brand portfolio constantly evolves, and we always search for new names to add. All items on our website are offered either directly by one of our jewelry partners or by the brand itself. You can follow the provided link to order directly from their website, or if you prefer, feel free to contact your nearest store to try them out in person. For added convenience, we also offer the service of transferring pre-selected items to the WatchDNA jeweller closest to you. While we strive to collaborate with local businesses, we also maintain strong relationships with certain manufacturers. Please be advised that for specific brands, we will redirect you directly to their own e-commerce platforms, and as a result, those brands cannot be physically tried in person. In addition, we take pride in making a positive impact on society, which is why we have partnered with exceptional nonprofits, and continually looking for additional local organizations to support. Get involved, discover the ideal timepiece or accessory, and help shape the world for future generations. TIME IS NOW! CREATE A WORLD FULL OF GIVING WITH US. "BE THE CHANGE YOU WANT TO SEE IN THE WORLD." GANDHI BE PART OF THE SOLUTION We all have our paths on this planet, and it’s our belief that discovering and pursuing our unique missions is vital to experiencing fulfillment. That’s why we’ve chosen to become part of B1G1 and 1% FOR THE PLANET ‘s global communities. By doing so, we aim to expand and enhance our community involvement as we journey through life. Living by our motto: Life is too short; one must enjoy it, Explore its kingdom in its immensity. Don't stop at what you believe to see, But on the contrary, search without any despair. Support our community Using Gandhi's quote as inspiration, we are firmly convinced that each of us can make a difference in our communities Fight climate change Our next generation deserves a better future, so we must work together to contribute to a Clean Earth. Check out below the non-for-profit organization that we decided to support. WatchDNA has committed to partnering with amazing non-profit organizations to support its mission of creating a world full of giving. Together, we could make a significant difference in the world and be part of the solution. For every transaction you make, something amazing happens in the world. TIME IS NOW! Watchmaking

pages.content: Watchmaking – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping WATCHMAKING 101 OUR GOAL IS TO CREATE A PLATFORM WHERE EVERYONE WHO WANTS TO LEARN ABOUT TIMEPIECES FEELS WELCOME We are here to help you on your quest to discover all things watches. With the expertise of watchmakers in our community we have produced an easy-to-understand glossary of watch terms to assist you in acquiring a better understanding of timepiece mechanics. Understanding the DNA of a watch can help you make informed decisions when it comes to choosing the right timepiece for you. Bezel The bezel is the ring surrounding the dial and crystal. Good to know: Some bezels rotate and are used for timing activities such as diving or measuring speed with a tachymeter scale . Crystal The crystal is the transparent cover that protects the dial while allowing you to read the time. Good to know: Most modern watches use sapphire crystal , which is highly scratch-resistant and extremely durable. Movement The movement is the internal mechanism that powers the watch and keeps time. Good to know: Movements can be mechanical (automatic or manual) or quartz , each offering different benefits in precision and craftsmanship. Bracelet or Strap The bracelet or strap is the band that holds the watch on your wrist. Good to know: Watches may come with metal bracelets, leather straps, rubber straps, or fabric , each suited for different styles and activities. Bezel The bezel is the ring surrounding the dial and crystal. Good to know: Some bezels rotate and are used for timing activities such as diving or measuring speed with a tachymeter scale . Crystal The crystal is the transparent cover that protects the dial while allowing you to read the time. Good to know: Most modern watches use sapphire crystal , which is highly scratch-resistant and extremely durable. Movement The movement is the internal mechanism that powers the watch and keeps time. Good to know: Movements can be mechanical (automatic or manual) or quartz , each offering different benefits in precision and craftsmanship. Bracelet or Strap The bracelet or strap is the band that holds the watch on your wrist. Good to know: Watches may come with metal bracelets, leather straps, rubber straps, or fabric , each suited for different styles and activities. Dial The dial is the face of the watch where the time is displayed with markers and details. Good to know: Dials can feature luminescent materials so you can read the time in low light. Hands The hands are the moving pointers that indicate the hours, minutes, and sometimes seconds. Good to know: Many watches use luminous coating on the hands so they glow in the dark. Crown The crown is the small knob on the side of the watch used to set the time and date. Good to know: On many sport watches, the crown is screw-down to help improve water resistance. Case The case is the main body of the watch that protects all the internal components. Good to know: Most quality watches use stainless steel, titanium, ceramic, or precious metals for durability and comfort. Dial The dial is the face of the watch where the time is displayed with markers and details. Good to know: Dials can feature luminescent materials so you can read the time in low light. pages.content: World Watch Day – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping World Watch Day “Let Time Unite Us. One Day. One Global Community.” On October 10, the world will celebrate World Watch Day, a day dedicated to the art, culture, and passion of watchmaking. From seasoned collectors to first-time enthusiasts, from master artisans to curious newcomers, this is a global invitation to pause, connect, and share in the timeless beauty of horology. Through local gatherings, online events, inspiring stories, and wrist shots shared across the globe, World Watch Day will highlight how watches are far more than instruments of timekeeping; they are symbols of heritage, innovation, and human creativity. Join the movement, discover the stories behind the timepieces, and be part of a worldwide celebration where every second counts. #WorldWatchDay ARTICLES ABOUT WORLD WATCH DAY WORLD WATCH DAY — WHY CELEBRATE TIMEPIECES? WORLD WATCH DAY LAUNCHES: A GLOBAL CELEBRATION ON 10/10 Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

RedBar
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: RedBar – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping RedBar Redbar, a globally recognized watch enthusiast group, began as a modest gathering in New York City and has evolved into an international community of horology lovers. Founded by Adam Craniotes , @Craniotes , and a few dedicated watch enthusiasts, Redbar's mission was to create a judgment-free, pretentious-free space for people to bond over their shared love for watches, enjoy drinks, and form lasting friendships. Initially, small, informal meetings were held at a bar that gave the group its name. Over time, the gatherings grew, and with the help of social media, Redbar's popularity soared, attracting members from various walks of life. Today, Redbar NYC meets monthly, inspiring the formation of chapters in cities like Toronto, London, Bombay, Edinburgh, and Dallas/Fort Worth. With over 85 chapters worldwide, Redbar continues to maintain its core values of inclusivity, camaraderie, and a shared passion for horology, offering a welcoming space for both seasoned collectors and new enthusiasts to explore the world of watches. Media Directory
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Media Directory – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Editorial Highlights and Curated Content Below, you’ll discover curated synopses of articles from respected media platforms. While the full articles are published on our partners’ websites, we proudly feature a brief overview here to help you explore their insights and perspectives. Each synopsis includes a direct link to the original publication. These collaborations are built on trust and shared values, as we continue working alongside media partners who help educate, inform, and inspire the global watch community. ‹ SOVRYGN WATCHES: A JOURNEY OF PASSION AND PERSEVERANCE February 24, 2026 GEORGE SULLY: FROM CANADIAN DESIGN ICON TO ADVOCATE AND STORYTELLER January 25, 2026 › Dedicated Watch Media Dedicated watch media are specialized publications focused exclusively on horology. They provide in-depth technical analysis, historical context, hands-on reviews, and industry insights tailored to collectors and enthusiasts. These outlets often shape collector opinion, influence brand perception, and serve as authoritative voices within the global watch community. A Timely Perspective A blog to watch Escapement Magazine Fratello Watches Hodinkee Monochrome Neue Uhren🇩🇪 Orafix🇮🇹 Passion horlogère🇫🇷 Quill & Pad Revolution Swiss Watches Magazine The Calibrated Wrist Time + Tide Time Square Magazine🇹🇼 Watch Collecting Lifestyle Watch Journal Watchonista Watch Pro Watch Time Watch Your Time Worn & Wound Wrist Enthusiast Lifestyle & fashion media Lifestyle & fashion media approach watches through the lens of design, style, and elevated living. Coverage emphasizes aesthetics, craftsmanship, brand storytelling, celebrity associations, and cultural relevance, positioning timepieces as symbols of achievement, refined taste, and personal identity. Rather than focusing purely on technical specifications, these outlets frame watches within the broader world of wealth, travel, automobiles, and fine living. Their readership—often composed of high-net-worth individuals and aspirational consumers—makes them powerful platforms for brand elevation, desirability, and lifestyle-driven visibility. BDC Magazine Collection Magazine Elle Elle Canada Elle Quebec🇫🇷 Fairmont Magazine Fashion Magazine Gear Patrol Globe & Mail Style magazine Glory Sports GQ Haute Living Hollywood Reporter Nuvo Magazine Robb Report Sharp Magazine The Kit Financial & Business Media Financial and business media analyze watches as economic assets and strategic industry players. Coverage may include market performance, brand acquisitions, resale value trends, and investment potential. These platforms position watches within the global luxury economy and appeal to executives, investors, and business-minded readers. Forbes Trade & Industry Media (B2B) Trade and industry publications serve professionals within the watch and jewelry ecosystem, including brands, authorized dealers, distributors, and suppliers. Their content focuses on retail strategy, distribution networks, product launches, trade shows, and regulatory developments. These outlets are essential for B2B visibility and industry credibility. Canadian Jeweller Haute Horlogerie Jewellery Business Federation of the Swiss Watch Industry Digital Creators & YouTube Media Digital creators and YouTube platforms provide accessible, personality

He is the author of four novels. × CAGDAS ONEN Watch Enthusiast & Founder of The Catalyst Welcome to The Catalyst with Cagdas Onen! In life, breaking through challenges and unlocking new possibilities requires a spark—what scientists call “Activation Energy.” Each episode is designed to ignite that spark by exploring diverse topics and hosting thought-provoking guests from around the globe. My mission is to inspire you, challenge your perspectives, and provide new pathways to help you achieve transformation in your personal and professional life. Think of me as your catalyst for change, helping you uncover opportunities that can lead to profound growth and success. Let’s embark on this journey together—one conversation at a time. Enjoy the ride! × CAROL BESLER Journalist Carol Besler has written and edited stories about watches and jewelry for Forbes, The Robb Report, Watch and Culture, Hollywood Reporter, Nuvo, Watch Time, Watch Journal, Journal Haute Horlogerie, The Globe and Mail, A Blog to Watch, Chronos (NA), Revolution, Watch Your Time (for The New York Times), Fashion Magazine, Haute living, Fairmont Magazine and Patek Philippe Magazine. She is the editorial Director of Tufts Custom Publishing, and was the editor-in-chief and publisher of Canadian Jeweller magazine for 16 years. × COLIN POTTS Horologist & Watch Enthusiast Colin is an avid watch enthusiast, collector, writer and horologist. He is the founder of the Jakob Eitan luxury watch

pages.title:
brands by Group
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: brands by Group – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping DISCOVER ALL WATCH GROUPS At WatchDNA, our mission is to Educate, Entertain, and Connect the global watch community. One of the first insights we realized is that many people aren’t aware that most watch brands are part of larger groups—each bringing together a variety of brands, expertise, and resources under one umbrella. This page is dedicated to helping you better understand these watch groups and their role in shaping the industry. "We hope you learn something new—and enjoy the journey through the world of watches." CASIO COMPUTER CO. LTD Casio, founded in 1946, became a digital watch pioneer with the 1974 CASIOTRON and cemented its legacy through iconic innovations like G-Shock, Pro Trek, Edifice, and the luxury-focused Oceanus. CITIZEN WATCH CO. LTD Citizen Watch Co. Ltd., founded in 1918, has grown into a global horological powerhouse known for Eco-Drive technology and innovation, with a diverse portfolio that includes Citizen, Bulova, Accutron, Frederique Constant, Alpina, Campanola, and Arnold & Son. COMPAGNIE FINANCIÈRE RICHEMONT SA Compagnie Financière Richemont SA, founded in 1988 by Johann Rupert, is a global luxury powerhouse that owns a diverse portfolio of historic watch and jewelry Maisons, including Cartier , Vacheron Constantin , and IWC , and is renowned for preserving craftsmanship while embracing innovation. FESTINA GROUP The Festina Group , a Spanish watch conglomerate founded in 1984, owns and operates several global watch brands—including Festina , Jaguar , Candino , Lotus , and Calypso —offering a range of Swiss-made and fashion-forward timepieces across various price points, with a strong heritage in sports marketing and cycling. FOSSIL GROUP INC The Fossil Group, founded in 1984, has grown into one of the world’s largest watch manufacturers, producing both in-house and licensed timepieces for brands like Armani Exchange , Diesel , DKNY , Kate Spade , Michele , Zodiac , and Skagen , each representing a unique design identity and market segment. FRANCK MULLER GROUP The Franck Muller Group, though small in size, is a respected Swiss watchmaking powerhouse that includes Franck Muller, Pierre Kunz, and Backes & Strauss, each known for bold designs, technical mastery, and distinctive identities. INVICTA WATCH GROUP Invicta Watch Group traces its origins to 1837, when Raphael Picard founded the company in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. Originally dedicated to crafting high-quality mechanical watches at accessible prices, the brand’s name— Invicta , meaning “invincible” in Latin—has long represented resilience and innovation in watchmaking. LVMH | Louis Vuitton Moët Hennessy LVMH, while best known for fashion, champagne, and spirits, has become a major force in watchmaking through its ownership of innovative and iconic brands like TAG Heuer , Tiffany & Co , Hublot , Zenith , Bulgari , Chaumet , and Daniel Roth . MONDAINE GROUP The Mondaine Group is a family-owned Swiss watchmaker that oversees four distinctive brands—Mondaine, Luminox, Pierre Cardin, and M-Watch—each tailored to different audiences, from iconic rail-inspired designs to rugged tactical gear and affordable fashion watches. MOVADO GROUP INC. The Movado Group Inc. is a leading global watch company that strategically combines heritage and fashion throu

pages.title:
Platforms
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Platforms – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping EXPLORE WATCH EDUCATION PLATFORMS Across the global watchmaking landscape, numerous independent platforms are dedicated to educating, documenting, and preserving horological knowledge. These resources offer valuable insights into watch history, craftsmanship, technical skills, and industry standards, helping both enthusiasts and professionals expand their understanding of the craft. This page highlights key educational platforms that contribute to the growth of the watch community by fostering learning, promoting transparency, and making expert information accessible worldwide. Switzerland HOROPEDIA Horopedia is a free, independent online video encyclopaedia dedicated to the art, science and culture of watchmaking, governed by the Horopedia Foundation , a Swiss public-interest non-profit organisation based in Geneva. The platform aims to preserve, document and share horological knowledge — from technical skills and historical context to core principles and professional practice — with students, professionals and enthusiasts worldwide. SWISS PRODIG Swiss Prodig is a professional Swiss industrial directory and B2B networking platform designed to connect manufacturers, subcontractors and service providers across high-precision sectors such as horology, micro-mechanics, MedTech, aerospace, electronics and industrial engineering. The platform allows companies to showcase their profiles, compare capabilities and engage directly with qualified partners for collaborative projects. HOROPEDIA The mission of the Horopedia Foundation includes educating and supporting understanding of watchmaking through rich multimedia content; producing and curating over 100 multilingual documentary films on museums, watchmaking schools, tools, components and mechanical principles; and maintaining nearly 1,000 detailed glossary pages explaining technical terms, mechanisms and historical developments. Content is provided in French, English and German. Learn more SWISS PRODIG The platform is operated by Swiss Prodig Sàrl, a limited liability company headquartered in La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland, with a business focus on creating, managing and developing a digital ecosystem for industrial supply-chain visibility and collaboration. Swiss Prodig’s mission is to make the Swiss industrial and subcontracting landscape more transparent, efficient and accessible by curating a reliable, manually verified directory of companies and by offering advanced search filters, comparison tools and direct contact functionalities that streamline partner identification for buyers and project managers. Learn more 1 OF 2 While not all groups are currently featured on our site, our directory is continually evolving as we expand and update our listings. Please feel free to reach out if you would like your organization to be listed, or if you are a member of an organization that supports industry stakeholders. pages.title:
Committee
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Committee – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping DISCOVER WATCH INDUSTRY COMMITTEES Within the global watchmaking ecosystem, several committees, associations, and organizations play a vital role in supporting local brands and industry stakeholders. These institutions help companies connect within their own countries and internationally, participate in global trade shows, strengthen industry visibility, and promote national craftsmanship on the world stage. This page introduces some of the key committees and associations dedicated to empowering watch brands and fostering cross-border collaboration. Canada CANADIAN JEWELLERS ASSOCIATION Canadian Jewellers Association (CJA) is the national trade association representing the Canadian jewellery and watch industry. Its origins date back to the early 20th century, when professionals in the field sought a unified voice to strengthen and protect the trade in Canada. CANADIAN JEWELLERY GROUP Canadian Jewellery Group (CJG) is Canada’s largest jewellery buying cooperative, empowering independent jewellers through collective purchasing and shared resources since its establishment in 1977. The organization was formed by seven jewellers from northern Ontario who collaborated to improve their purchasing power and share the benefits of negotiated supplier terms, rebates, and discounts with members across the country. CANADIAN JEWELLERS ASSOCIATION The organization traces its roots to the Jewellers Security Alliance of Canada, founded in 1884 to help improve security practices and combat theft. This initiative later evolved into the Canadian National Jewellers Association, which held its first official meeting on September 25, 1918 . By 1921, the Association had introduced its first Code of Ethics, and in 1922 it formally adopted the name Canadian Jewellers Association (CJA) . Since then, the CJA has remained committed to education, advocacy, and promoting ethical business standards across the jewellery and watch sectors. The CJA is recognized as a key authority within the Canadian jewellery and watch trade, guiding members through its Code of Ethics, educational programs, and advocacy efforts. Its work supports industry growth, strengthens consumer confidence, and ensures best practices across all areas of the jewellery sector. Learn more CANADIAN JEWELLERY GROUP Over the decades, the CJG has grown into a nationally recognized group with retail members operating numerous jewellery stores throughout Canada. By harnessing the collective buying strength of its members, the Group provides access to premium products from North America’s top suppliers while allowing independent retailers to maintain their unique identities and local presence. The organization negotiates favorable terms and conditions with endorsed suppliers and passes 100% of those advantages on to its members, including volume rebates, exclusive payment options, and centralized billing handled by the CJG head office. It also hosts an annual buying show and facilitates networking, education, and collaboration opportunities for its membership, reinforcing the competitive position of independent jewellers in the national market. Learn more 1 OF 2 France FRANCECLAT Francéclat is the professional committee dedicated to supporting and promoting France’s watchmaking, jewelry, and tableware industries—

pages.title: WatchDNA | Your Daily Routine
pages.content: WatchDNA | Your Daily Routine Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Your Daily Routine What started as a photography project—rooted in our founder’s first passion for capturing the daily lives of strangers he crossed paths with—has now evolved into something even more meaningful. You may have seen some of his early work on his personal account, @LiveandCapture . We’re now inviting you to be part of this growing story: Share a photo of your wrist and the timepiece you’re wearing, along with your location and the date. This simple gesture allows us—and our community—to “pause” time for just a moment. It’s a powerful reminder of how quickly life moves and how meaningful it is when we take time to reflect, connect, and create something together. Let’s come together and showcase the beauty of shared moments and collective action—one wristshot at a time. ✨ And if you’d like, feel free to add a personal comment with your submission—we’d love to hear what time means to you. ⏱️ Want to go even deeper? If you wish to participate more fully in this project, we welcome you to reach out and submit a short article about how timepieces and the act of keeping time play a role in your daily routine and everyday life. Your story might be featured in an upcoming post or article. Discover our community post #WatchDNADailyRoutine CAPTURES FROM OUR COMMUNITY Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

pages.content: B1g1 business for good – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping B1g1 business for good THE STORY OF B1G1 In 2007, B1G1 started with a simple idea: “ What if every business could make a difference in their own way, just by doing what they normally do? ”. With more than a decade of work, this simple idea has now become a global movement. TOTAL GIVING IMPACTS WHAT ARE THE SDGS? On 25 September 2015, world leaders from all sectors convened to adopt the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). They represent the global community’s next step in ending poverty, fighting inequality and injustice, and tackling climate change for the next 15 years. Here at B1G1, we feel motivated by these collective indicators too. There is now a greater call for businesses to take much-needed actions. LEARN MORE TIME IS NOW! pages.content: David Carrington – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping David Carrington "David Carrington is the Founder and CEO of COMPASS Timepieces, a purpose-driven luxury watch brand rooted in Swiss precision and a modern legacy mindset. Inspired from a young age growing up in New York City, David questioned the absence of Black-owned brands within the luxury watch space — a realization that would later shape his mission to create meaningful timepieces that stand for ownership, individuality, and purpose. After building a career in IT, David made the decisive leap in 2022 to pursue his lifelong passion for horology. He began developing COMPASS alongside a Swiss manufacturing team in La Chaux-de-Fonds, committing fully to the brand after a career layoff reinforced his calling to go all in. Today, COMPASS Timepieces crafts watches with Swiss ETA movements and premium materials, each designed to mark personal milestones and encourage collectors to “Find Purpose™” in every moment." Discover the brand at compasstimepieces.com DAVID'S ARTICLES FINDING PURPOSE IN TIME: BUILDING LUXURY TIMEPIECES WITH INTENTION Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Based in Toronto, with an impressive worldwide network, Sully has established a collective of multifaceted companies that leverage him as an innovative player in the world of multimedia content creation and fashion design. Celebrated CAFA Change-maker Award winner , George Brown honorary degree recipient and FGI Visionary Award Winner, Sully is also known for his collaborations that include but are not limited to DHL, EBAY, Disney, Hudson’s Bay, and Mercedes Benz. Sully is also a Bata Shoe Museum inductee, creator of Black Designers of Canada , co-founder of House of Hayla , and creator of the breakout brand Sully & Son Company . He loves to share his entrepreneurial experiences and his sense of fashion, inspiring others to pursue their passions and embrace their unique styles. pages.content: GIAN-PAOLO MAZZOTTA, Tailor, Designer, Stylist & Watch Enthusiat – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping GIAN-PAOLO MAZZOTTA Gian-Paolo Mazzotta is an accomplished tailor, designer, and stylist, dedicated to creating the finest clothing for a quality lifestyle. With over two decades in the fashion industry, this Toronto designer has been recognized by Sharp Magazine and BlogTO as one of the best bespoke and made-to-measure tailors in Toronto, most recently in January 2024. Gian-Paolo established and managed a street-level store in Toronto’s trendy Queen West for five years, where he honed his craft and developed his unique sensibilities. His career further includes leading the bespoke department at Holt Renfrew, refining his expertise in luxury fashion. Currently, as the head tailor of ILLI Bespoke and The Military Tailor, he serves an impressive clientele, including executives, entrepreneurs, academics, national leaders, royal representatives, colonels, naval captains, actors, and athletes. Gian-Paolo has dressed prominent figures like Manjit Minhas, Stephen Curry, DVSN, Virginia to Vegas and the Lieutenant Governor of Ontario. His diverse experience ensures each garment is both luxurious and personal, tailored to the body and identity of each individual. pages.title:
HAKIM EL KADIRI Founder of ELKA Watch Co.
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: HAKIM EL KADIRI Founder of ELKA Watch Co. – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping HAKIM EL KADIRI “After 25 years working for the watch industry, I think it was time to fly by my own and reinvent myself! I have always admired entrepreneurs, people who created a company, who made it grow and who lived this role with passion, I myself was what is called an intrapreneur, it’s a priori the same thing, it’s a function that allows you to take decisions with responsibilities, that allows you to develop with passion, because you are fully involved in your projects – but I did it in a large group; which gives us a lot of room for manoeuvre in a certain sense. I dreamt of developing my own brand but above all I wanted a brand with a story, truth & credibility. I chose to call it by my nickname, ELKA ; When I wanted to protect the name, I realised that a brand called Elka Watch had already existed but had disappeared around the 60s & 70s precisely during the timeframe that I have chosen to base my designs on. This defunct Elka Watch was named after Eduard Louis Kiek from Amsterdam. I looked through its archives and realised that the brand had strong designs and had worked closely with other big names in the watch industry such as Ulysse Nardin, Heuer, Rolex, Minerva, to name only a few. So, without reviving the brand, I wanted to pay a tribute to this old Dutch brand with my first 2 series. Since I love the 60s, the products and the aesthetics of that era I have decided to make it my design line for this and future collections. End of the 60’s was the beginning of the design of modernity, everything was allowed without restriction, because everything was to be created, pop art, cars, fashion, architecture, television, interior design, music, space conquest… In this period, there was a diversity of forms, of ideas, the creators did not have any restriction except in technologies and materials which were not all mastered yet” Elkawatch.ch HAKIM EL KADIRI'S ARTICLES A NEO-VINTAGE REVIVAL BLENDING HISTOR WITH MODERN CRAFTSMANSHIP ELKA WATCH CO.: CRAFTING TIME, AN INTERVIEW WITH HAKIM EL KADIRI A LARGE PART OF THE DESIGN LIES IN THE SHAPE OF THE SAPPHIRE CRYSTAL Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Opens in a new window.

Jacky is an artist, watchmaker, and watch enthusiast whose creative universe is deeply shaped by American science-fiction films and series. With a foundation in textile engineering and fashion design, he crafts mixed-media artworks that fuse fabric, paint, and 3D printing into immersive, story-driven collections. Beyond visual art, Jacky also provides custom watch services—treating each timepiece as a miniature canvas for personal expression. His contributions to WatchDNA explore boundary-breaking watch design, highlighting pieces that blur the line between wearable art, futuristic concepts, and traditional horology. 🚀 A creator at heart, Jacky pushes the limits of imagination—one design at a time. This sparked an enduring love for Omega watches, including the Speedmaster, Constellations, and Seamasters, leading to a diverse collection featuring over 20 Omegas, IWC, vintage Longines, Nivada, and Tissot. As the head of the Toronto Chapter of RedBar, Mark has found a vibrant community that shares his enthusiasm, enabling him to connect with like-minded individuals, explore new brands, and dive deeper into the world of watchmaking. She has been a pioneer in the watch world since 1984, leading the way for four decades for women in this industry. She is an award-winning journalist who has authored six books on watches, a decade worth of annual books on watches, and contributed chapters to many brand books. She has built a reputation for creating cutting-edge, behind-the-scenes watch articles and celebrity interviews, contributing to a host of publications such as Forbes, the International New York Times, Bloomberg, and others. She is also the founder of America’s first female owned and operated watch platform, ATimelyPerspective.com , where she continues to bring her unique perspective on watches to the forefront today. pages.content: Spiro Mandylor – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Spiro Mandylor Spiro Mandylor has spent over a decade photographing fashion in Toronto, New York, Los Angeles, and Miami. In 2012, he became the first Canadian (and Greek) Olympus Photography Visionary Photographer. an honor bestowed upon a select group of photographers chosen to serve as official brand ambassadors. That same year, he joined E! Celebrity Style Story as one of their television style panelists. During the inaugural season, he contributed to celebrity style profiles of Sandra Bullock, Julianne Moore, Johnny Depp, Jessica Alba, Faith Hill, Kelly Osbourne, and many others. Spiro Mandylor’s work has been published in online and print magazines such as Luxsure Paris, Bisous Los Angeles, TCHAD Magazine, and NAME New York. His photography has been featured in exhibitions including Scotiabank CONTACT, the PDN PhotoPlus Exhibit, and Exposure. He has collaborated with international brands on digital content projects, including IMG, Nikon, World MasterCard, GUESS Watches, London Olympics, Michael Kors, Diesel, Lacoste, Ted Baker, Volvo, CIBC, General Motors, Jaguar Land Rover, Honda Motors, Acura, and NBC. pages.title:
THOMAS BRISSIAUD
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: THOMAS BRISSIAUD – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping THOMAS BRISSIAUD "My name is Thomas and in 2024 I created Tessé Watches with the vision of crafting a meticulously designed vintage-inspired timepiece as a tribute to my grandfather, Michel. My aspiration was to produce a distinctive watch that embodied the essence of timeless craftsmanship, all while delivering exceptional value for my customers. This endeavor laid the foundation for our brand, setting the stage for continuous growth and the development of new models. As a modest, independent company fueled by a passion for horology, our commitment revolves around meticulous attention to detail, captivating design and the creation of truly unique timepieces. We reject the notion of merely replicating well-known watch designs. Instead we strive to infuse our creations with personal depth and individually in a saturated market of micro-brands. Our inaugural series reimagines classic designs, designed to be worn daily, with an aesthetic that we believe will resonate across generations. Early-on, I decided to partner with Swiss manufacturers. From conceptualization to manufacturing, we scrutinize every aspect of our watches to ensure the best possible value for our customers. Embracing a direct-to-consumer approach from the outset, we conscientiously control expenses that do not directly give value to our customers." Sandrin, MBA Thomas J. Sandrin, MBA a luxury watch industry veteran with 16+ years of experience, relocated from Paris to Canada. He excelled as canadian brand manager for Hamilton watch , part of Swatch Group, and holds an MBA from the Australian Institute of Business. Leader with a proven track record of success, Thomas believes in the power of networking. He has a deep-seated conviction that it is essential to cultivate meaningful connections with others. In addition, Thomas is always happy to share his knowledge and expertise while meeting wonderful people and acquiring new skills and insights. He places a high priority on maintaining a professional and trustworthy reputation among all of his peers. In 2021, Thomas J. Sandrin decided to launch Northern Watch Services Inc. , a 360° agency dedicated to providing authorized jewellers and luxury brands with industry expertise to enhance their brand presence online and through in-person activations. The company's motto is "Connecting Jewellers with Brands, Watch Enthusiasts, and Corporate Clients.” Then, throughout his MBA journey and after extensive market research discussions with many stakeholders within the industry—retailers, brands, and end-consumers—he decided to launch WatchDNA to educate watch aficionados and connect them with a network of jewellers. pages.content: Tyler Worden – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Tyler Worden "Tyler Worden is an Industrial Designer and founder of Worden Watch Studio , a design-forward microbrand creating expressive and soulful timepieces that reflect our shared human experience. With an artist’s obsession for meaning and purpose, Tyler built Worden to bridge the gap he saw in the watch industry — creating watches that feel intentional, poetic, and connected to life’s emotional rhythms, not just functional accessories. After living and traveling in cities like Montreal and Seoul, and ultimately studying Industrial Design at Carleton University, Tyler combined his creative instincts with technical skill to design watches that feel personal and expressive. He believes watches should first honor "usability, dependability, and comfort", and then push artistic boundaries that give wearers a sense of identity and connection. At Worden, this philosophy becomes tangible in each limited edition release, where bold, story-driven concepts materialize as wrist-sculptures rather than conventional designs. His work challenges the conservative norms of mainstream watchmaking and invites a new generation of collectors to join a movement that values authenticity over replication. Explore his vision and work at wordenwatch.com/about TYLER'S ARTICLES WORDEN WATCH STUDIO: WATCHES AS SMALL CREATIVE UNIVERSES Back to top Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. Over the years, it has grown into one of the most prestigious events in the watchmaking world, bringing together the finest maisons and industry professionals. In 2020, it was rebranded as Watches and Wonders, expanding its global footprint with the addition of Watches and Wonders Shanghai. pages.title: WatchDNA official Media Partner of the Timepiece Show "Canada"
pages.content: WatchDNA official Media Partner of the Timepiece Show "Canada" Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Timepiece Show WatchDNA is proud to be a media partner of the Timepiece Shows, an exceptional event that brings together luxury and vintage watch enthusiasts, collectors, and dealers in Canada. As a trusted platform for watch lovers, we are excited to support this gathering, which celebrates horological craftsmanship and provides unique opportunities for watch aficionados to meet. WatchDNA looks forward to sharing the passion and excitement of the event with our community, while connecting with industry experts and fellow watch enthusiasts. While we’re excited for the upcoming Toronto Timepiece Show, we’re already gearing up for an incredible experience in Vancouver. That’s why we’ve dedicated this page to covering all things Canada. Time to Watches
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Time to Watches – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Time to Watches Since its launch in 2022, Time to Watches has established itself as one of the most dynamic international watch events, uniting luxury watch brands, Swiss manufacturers, independent watchmakers, and microbrands with their global audiences. More than just a trade show, it is a platform for product launches, brand storytelling, professional networking, and authentic engagement with collectors, retailers, media, influencers, and watch enthusiasts. The flagship Swiss edition is held every April during Geneva Watch Week, while the U.S. edition in Las Vegas each May focuses on the North American market. From haute horlogerie to avant-garde creations, Time to Watches fosters an atmosphere that blends innovation, craftsmanship, and authenticity. With its informal yet professional style, the event highlights product excellence, creativity, and meaningful connections that extend beyond traditional boundaries. Whether you are a watch brand seeking visibility, a collector discovering the next hidden gem, or a retailer exploring opportunities, Time to Watches is where the global watch industry connects, inspires, and evolves. The awards recognize timepiece design achievement in 11 different categories and an additional people's choice category for the finalists of all the categories. The finalist grouping of each category and winning selection of each finalist group are determined by a confidential vote of the selected Timepiece World award 2025 committee members and jurors. The 2025 list of award-winners will be announced at the 1st annual Timepiece World awards ceremony that will take place on Saturday, September 20, 2025 in Toronto, Canada. Now in its 12th edition, the awards honor excellence across 12 diverse categories, including sustainable, connected, complication, and everyday watches. What sets TIA apart is its unique hybrid model: a panel of international experts selects the finalists, while the global public casts votes online to determine the winners. The 2025 edition will take place in Zurich on October 2, with voting already open for the “Masterpiece” category, which highlights standout pieces from Watches & Wonders Geneva. pages.title:
Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG)
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) Founded in 2001, the Grand Prix d’Horlogerie de Genève (GPHG) is often called “the Oscars of watchmaking,” celebrating the year’s most exceptional creations and promoting watchmaking art worldwide. Held every November in Geneva, it features around 20 awards across categories such as Ladies’, Men’s, Chronograph, Tourbillon, Sports, Jewellery, Artistic Crafts, and Iconic, plus special prizes like Petite Aiguille, Eco-Innovation, and Horological Revelation. Finalists are shortlisted by the GPHG Academy and judged by a 30-member international jury of experts. The 2024 edition, hosted at Geneva’s Théâtre du Léman, saw IWC’s Portugieser Eternal Calendar win the Aiguille d’Or, Van Cleef & Arpels sweep the women’s categories, and independents like Rémy Cools and Ming take top honors. Chopard also earned the inaugural Eco-Innovation prize, reflecting the GPHG’s growing focus on sustainability. Blending tradition and innovation, GPHG remains the premier annual celebration of fine watchmaking.  – WatchDNA
pages.content: The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition The 42nd Hong Kong Watch & Clock Design Competition, organized by the HKTDC alongside the Hong Kong Watch Manufacturers Association and the Federation of Hong Kong Watch Trades & Industries, runs concurrently with the 2025 Hong Kong Watch & Clock Fair and invites talent to shine in two categories—an Open Group (for individual designers or Hong Kong-registered companies) and a Student Group—each inspired by distinct themes (“Memorable” for Open and “Believe In Yourself” for Student). The competition offers winners up to HK$15,000, trophies, certificates, watch courses, media exposure, and opportunities to launch their designs to market. With applications closing on 30 April 2025, the evaluation process includes initial judging in mid-May, mock-up production through June–July, final judging in mid-July, culminating in an award presentation in early September—making it a dynamic platform to celebrate creativity, innovation, and emerging horological talent in Hong Kong. pages.title:
FAQ'S
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: FAQ'S – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping FAQ'S FREQUENTLY ASKED QUESTIONS What is WatchDNA.com, and what does it offer? WatchDNA.com is a global platform that connects the watch community by offering a comprehensive directory of watch brands, authorized dealers, and accessories. It also features educational content, watch reviews, store spotlights, and interviews with industry leaders. Our goal is to educate, entertain, and connect watch enthusiasts, collectors, and brands worldwide. Whether you’re looking for a new timepiece, discovering emerging brands, or learning about the latest trends, WatchDNA.com is your trusted resource for all things watches. Why can I not buy directly on WatchDNA site? WatchDNA is designed as a platform to bridge the gap between watch enthusiasts, brands, and their network of Authorized Dealers. We share a deep passion for timepieces and the unique stories behind their owners. We believe strongly in fostering trust and supporting local businesses, which play an essential role in their communities. That’s why WatchDNA is built as a commission-free platform , enabling you—the watch enthusiast—to connect directly with Authorized Dealers, engage with them, and purchase with confidence. This approach not only strengthens local retailers but also ensures that you benefit from expert guidance and authentic service, while discovering your next timepiece from those who best understand the craftsmanship and value behind every watch. Does WatchDNA offer watch certification? While we are not responsible for the authenticity of these timepieces, we only showcase products available through our trusted network of business partners. In addition, we are proud to collaborate with Watch Certificate™ , which, through their network of certified watchmakers, provides an additional layer of trust and assurance for your timepieces. To learn more about Watch Certificate™, you can connect directly with Trillium Watch Service. Are all watch brands featured on WatchDNA? At WatchDNA, we value informed decisions when purchasing timepieces. While not all brands are available within our jeweller network, we’re happy to connect you directly with brands that sell exclusively online. If you’re searching for a brand or model not featured on our site, simply reach out , and we’ll do our best to connect you with a trusted jeweller partner or the brand itself. Our selection is constantly growing, and we look forward to being part of your journey to the perfect timepiece. Your quest for the perfect timepiece is a journey we're excited to be a part of. I already buy watches from my local store. What could WatchDNA bring me? We're delighted to hear you've established a trusted relationship with your local jeweler— supporting local businesses is at the heart of what we do at WatchDNA. If your favorite store isn't yet featured on our platform, we'd be honored if you could introduce us. Our aim is to ensure they can extend their high-quality service to more local shoppers like you through our site. Additionally, WatchDNA is continually updated with the latest news from brands and captivating stories within our industry. We invite you to visit our site regularly to stay informed and connected with the world of timmepieces. When a new jeweller joins our platform, we make a contribution to a worthy cause,

pages.title:
Discover the World of Watches
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: Discover the World of Watches – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping Discover The World Of Watches We created this section to make exploring the universe of watches simple, intuitive, and engaging. Through easy-to-read cards, you can navigate key concepts, styles, and knowledge without feeling overwhelmed. Whether you’re just getting started or deepening your expertise, everything is organized into clear categories designed to guide your journey through time. DID YOU KNOW? A quick, fun way to learn something new about watches. From surprising facts to hidden details, this section delivers bite-sized insights that spark curiosity and deepen your appreciation for horology—one discovery at a time. YOUR WATCH TELLS MORE THAN TIME? IT REFLECTS YOUR STYLE, STORY, AND LIFESTYLE. WHAT AUTOMATIC MEANS? IT’S POWERED BY YOUR WRIST MOVEMENT. NO BATTERY NEEDED. SWISS MADE IS REGULATED? AT LEAST 60% OF THE VALUE MUST BE SWISS. YOUR FIRST WATCH SHOULD FIT YOUR LIFESTYLE? DAILY WEAR > HYPE. AUTHORIZED DEALERS PROTECT YOUR WARRANTY? WATCH ACCURACY IS MEASURED IN SECONDS PER DAY? MECHANICAL WATCHES VARY NATURALLY. STEEL TYPES MATTER? 316L STEEL RESISTS CORROSION AND SKIN REACTIONS. QUARTZ WATCHES USE BATTERIES? YES! THEY’RE ACCURATE, AFFORDABLE, AND LOW-MAINTENANCE. BRACELET QUALITY MATTERS? SOLID LINKS FEEL HEAVIER AND LAST LONGER. WATER-RESISTANT ≠ WATERPROOF? ALWAYS CHECK THE METERS RATING BEFORE SWIMMING. WATCH LUME HELPS YOU READ IN THE DARK? SUPER-LUMINOVA GLOWS AFTER LIGHT EXPOSURE. NATO STRAPS WERE MADE FOR THE MILITARY? DURABLE, WASHABLE, AND SUPER VERSATILE. WATCHES HAVE SHOCK PROTECTION? SYSTEMS LIKE INCABLOC PROTECT THE MOVEMENT. LEATHER STRAPS NEED CARE? AVOID WATER AND ROTATE STRAPS TO LAST LONGER. A ROTATING BEZEL CAN TRACK TIME? ESPECIALLY USEFUL FOR DIVERS. HAND-WOUND WATCHES NEED DAILY WINDING? NO ROTOR, JUST YOU AND THE CROWN. SKELETON WATCHES SHOW THE MECHANICS? STYLE MEETS ENGINEERING. LUG-TO-LUG IS MORE IMPORTANT THAN CASE SIZE? IT DETERMINES HOW THE WATCH SITS ON YOUR WRIST. CHRONOGRAPH ≠ CHRONOMETER? CHRONOGRAPH = STOPWATCH. CHRONOMETER = ACCURACY CERTIFIED. QUICK-RELEASE STRAPS SAVE TIME? NO TOOLS NEEDED TO CHANGE STRAPS. MECHANICAL WATCHES NEED SERVICING? EVERY 2–3 YEARS TO STAY ACCURATE. SAPPHIRE CRYSTAL IS SCRATCH-RESISTANT? IT’S MUCH HARDER THAN MINERAL GLASS. GMT WATCHES TRACK MULTIPLE TIME ZONES? PERFECT FOR TRAVELERS EXHIBITION CASEBACKS SHOW THE MOVEMENT? PERFECT FOR WATCH LOVERS 100M WATER RESISTANCE IS FOR SWIMMING? YES, BUT NOT FOR DEEP DIVING. WATCH WEIGHT AFFECTS COMFORT? LIGHTER WATCHES FEEL BETTER FOR ALL-DAY WEAR. LIMITED EDITIONS CAN HOLD VALUE? ESPECIALLY WHEN DEMAND IS HIGHER THAN SUPPLY. A DATE WINDOW NEEDS MANUAL ADJUSTMENT? ESPECIALLY AFTER MONTHS WITH FEWER DAYS. MOONPHASE WATCHES SHOW THE LUNAR CYCLE? A POETIC COMPLICATION WITH REAL HISTORY. GOLD WATCHES COME IN ALLOYS? PURE GOLD IS TOO SOFT FOR DAILY WEAR. IN-HOUSE MOVEMENTS AREN’T ALWAYS BETTER? RELIABILITY MATTERS MORE THAN MARKETING. CERAMIC WATCHES DON’T SCRATCH EASILY? THEY KEEP THEIR SHINE FOR YEARS. VINTAGE-INSPIRED WATCHES ARE TRENDING? OLD-SCHOOL DESIGN, MODERN TECH. POWER RESERVE TELLS YOU HOW LONG IT RUNS? SOME WATCHES UP TO 72+ HOURS OFF THE WRIST. WATCH BOXES PROTECT VALUE? KEEP THE FULL SET IF YOU EVER RESELL. CASE SIZE AFFECTS COMFORT? BIGGER ISN’T ALWAYS BETTER, FIT MATTERS. WATCHES ARE MECHA

pages.title:
TRADESHOWS
 – WatchDNA
pages.content: TRADESHOWS – WatchDNA Skip to content BUILDING A COMMUNITY OF WATCH LOVERS WE AIM TO BECOME YOUR TRUSTED DIRECTORY FOR ALL THINGS WATCHES Item added to your cart View cart Check out Continue shopping DISCOVER WATCH INDUSTRY TRADESHOW From global exhibitions to specialized fairs, watch industry tradeshows serve as essential platforms for brands to showcase their craftsmanship, connect with international buyers, and stay ahead of emerging market trends. This page highlights some of the most influential events shaping the future of watchmaking worldwide. UPCOMING TRADESHOWS Consumer Watch Events (B2C) Professional Trade Events (B2B) Know of a trade show we're missing? Send it our way! × Canada CJA - EXPO PRESTIGE Expo Prestige is Canada’s longest-running and most prestigious jewellery trade show, bringing together retailers, suppliers, manufacturers, and industry professionals for in-person buying, networking, and business opportunities. Organized by the Canadian Jewellers Association, Expo Prestige offers a focused trade environment where buyers can discover new collections, meet key brands, and strengthen business relationships. THE CANADIAN WATCHES & JEWELRY SHOW The Canadian Watches & Jewelry Show is a one-day industry and enthusiast event held in Laval, near Montreal, bringing together leading exhibitors of luxury timepieces and fine jewelry. Designed for both seasoned collectors and curious newcomers, the show offers a curated showcase of watch brands and jewelry designers, highlighting craftsmanship, design, and innovation. TIME & SHINE Time & Shine Expo is Canada’s trade-only jewellery marketplace where verified jewellers and online jewellers come together to source products, build supplier relationships, and write orders for the upcoming season. In 2026, the event takes place in multiple major Canadian cities and provides a business-focused environment to discover collections, compare suppliers, and connect with key industry partners. TIMEPIECE SHOW WatchDNA is proud to be a media partner of the Timepiece Shows , an exceptional event that brings together luxury and vintage watch enthusiasts, collectors, and dealers in Canada. As a trusted platform for watch lovers, we are excited to support this gathering, which celebrates horological craftsmanship and provides unique opportunities for watch aficionados to meet. CJA - EXPO PRESTIGE Expo Prestige is Canada’s longest-running and most prestigious jewellery trade show, bringing together retailers, suppliers, manufacturers, and industry professionals for in-person buying, networking, and business opportunities. Organized by the Canadian Jewellers Association, Expo Prestige offers a focused trade environment where buyers can discover new collections, meet key brands, and strengthen business relationships. Expo Prestige features a three-day trade show format with onsite buyer registration, curated exhibitor displays, and opportunities for retailers to connect with top jewellery producers and service providers in the industry. Learn more THE CANADIAN WATCHES & JEWELRY SHOW The Canadian Watches & Jewelry Show is a one-day industry and enthusiast event held in Laval, near Montreal, bringing together leading exhibitors of luxury timepieces and fine jewelry. Designed for both seasoned collectors and curious newcomers, the show offers a curated showcase of watch brands and jewelry designers, highlighting craftsmanship, design, and innovation. It serves as a key moment for the Canadian watch and jewelry community to connect, discover new collections, and celebrate the artistry behind horology and fine accessories. This trade-only format ensures focused networking and high-value conversations designed to help jewellery professionals grow and succeed. Time & Shine Expo emphasizes quality buying opportunities, supplier meetings, exclusive show content, and education designed for jewellery business success.
